1320	### Re-queue audit event schema
1321	
1322	The `runRequeue` mutation reopens the originating bead and appends a
1323	`run_requeue` event to that bead's audit log. Concurrent or repeat
1324	submissions with the same `idempotencyKey` collapse to a single
1325	re-queue and a single event (`deduplicated=true` is returned to all
1326	subsequent callers); if the cached idempotency record points at a
1327	missing bead, the requeue is replayed against the run's current
1328	originating bead. The event uses the standard `bead.BeadEvent`
1329	envelope and the following field schema:
1330	
1331	| Field | Value |
1332	|---|---|
1333	| `kind` | `run_requeue` (`RunRequeueEventKind`) |
1334	| `summary` | `run requeued` |
1335	| `actor` | Resolved operator identity; falls back to `anonymous` when no request identity is available |
1336	| `source` | `graphql:runRequeue` |
1337	| `body` | Single line `key=value` payload: `identity=<identity.kind> actor=<identity.actor> run_id=<runId> idempotency_key=<key> layer_override=<layer-or-empty>` |
1338	
1339	The body line is structured for grep/jq parsing: each token is a
1340	`key=value` pair separated by single spaces. `layer_override` is the
1341	empty string when the caller did not pass `RunRequeueInput.layer`.
1342	`identity.kind` is the resolver-selected operator identity class
1343	(`unknown`, `localhost`, or `tsnet`). `actor` is the resolved user or
1344	operator label; when the request carries no identifying header, the
1345	audit fields fall back to `identity=unknown actor=anonymous`.
1346	
1347	The GraphQL mutation response may also report `deduplicated=true`, but
1348	that is a write-response flag, not part of the persisted audit event.
1349	
1350	Consumers of the audit log (FEAT-008 Runs row expansion, evaluation
1351	skills under FEAT-019) treat `run_requeue` events as the canonical
1352	record that a re-queue happened: there is no separate persisted
1353	`requeue` record beyond (a) the new `open` status on the originating
1354	bead and (b) this event entry.
1355	
1356	### Run-detail access audit events
1357	
1358	The first time the canonical project-scoped run detail is resolved for a
1359	run record that is visible through project membership, DDx appends a
1360	`run_detail_view` event to the originating bead's audit log. This is the
1361	canonical record that a human inspected the run-detail surface.
1362	
1363	The event is read-side only: it is emitted on the canonical detail load,
1364	not on in-page tab switches, evidence downloads, or breadcrumb/back
1365	navigation. If the caller cannot see the project, there is no run detail
1366	and therefore no audit event.
1367	
1368	The `body` line is structured for grep/jq parsing and includes the
1369	project-scoped identity of the inspection:
1370	
1371	| Field | Value |
1372	|---|---|
1373	| `kind` | `run_detail_view` |
1374	| `summary` | `run detail viewed` |
1375	| `actor` | Viewer identity derived from the authenticated project member |
1376	| `source` | `graphql:run` |
1377	| `body` | Single line: `project_id=<projectId> run_id=<runId> layer=<layer> visibility=project_membership` |
1378	
1379	### Run resolver contract
1380	
1381	The canonical deep loader for a run detail surface is `run(id:)`. It returns
1382	the run-layer fields used by FEAT-008:
1383	
1384	- `prompt`
1385	- `response`
1386	- `stderr`
1387	- `bundleFiles[]`
1388	
1389	The tool-call resolver is the `toolCalls(first, after)` stream exposed via
1390	`runToolCalls(id:, first:, after:)`. It returns the normalized tool-call
1391	stream as a paginated `RunToolCallConnection`, where each node projects the
1392	canonical drain-time `ToolCallEntry` shape:
1393	
1394	- `tool`
1395	- `input`
1396	- `output`
1397	- `duration_ms`
1398	- `error`
1399	
1400	`ToolCallEntry` is the normalized agent-side record of one tool execution. DDx
1401	persists the stream at drain time so the run substrate can page the stored
1402	sequence without rehydrating raw agent logs.
1403	
1404	The `bundleFile(path)` lookup is exposed via `runBundleFile(id:, path:)`. It
1405	is confined to the run's bundle root, rejects path traversal, absolute paths,
1406	and symlink escapes, and inlines content only for the whitelist of small text
1407	files documented by FEAT-008.
1408	
1409	### Layer-to-substrate mapping for the Runs UI
1410	
1411	The web Runs view (FEAT-008 §5, FEAT-021) renders three layer chips
1412	backed by the unified substrate plus the legacy detail backings. The
1413	shared run-detail tab strip is the canonical drill-down surface used by
1414	FEAT-008, FEAT-019, and FEAT-021:
1415	
1416	| Chip | Substrate row | Detail backing for row expansion |
1417	|---|---|---|
1418	| `work` | `layer: 3` Run record | Layer-3 record's queue inputs / stop-condition log / child layer-2 attempt ids; the shared tab strip exposes `overview` only |
1419	| `try` | `layer: 2` Run record | The `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/` bundle attached to the layer-2 record (manifest, prompt, result, checks, verdict); the shared tab strip exposes `overview`, `prompt`, `response`, `tools`, and `evidence` |
1420	| `run` | `layer: 1` Run record | The associated `AgentSession` row (prompt / response / stderr / billing / cached-token detail) joined onto the layer-1 record; the shared tab strip exposes `overview`, `prompt`, `response`, `session`, `tools`, and `evidence` |
1421	
1422	`AgentSession` rows that have no parent layer-2 attempt (raw `ddx
1423	agent log` invocations) surface as synthesized `layer=run` Runs rows
1424	keyed by session id, so no agent-session row is dropped during the
1425	Sessions/Executions tab retirement.
1426	
1427	The shared tab strip is:
1428	
1429	- `work` → `overview`
1430	- `try` → `overview`, `prompt`, `response`, `tools`, `evidence`
1431	- `run` → `overview`, `prompt`, `response`, `session`, `tools`, `evidence`
1432	
1433	## User Stories
1434	
1435	### US-090: Developer Inspects a Layer-1 Run
1436	
1437	**As a** developer running an ad-hoc agent invocation
1438	**I want** to inspect the resulting run record
1439	**So that** I can audit prompt, response, tokens, and side-effects
1440	
1441	**Acceptance Criteria:**
1442	- Given I invoke `ddx run`, then DDx writes one layer-1 record under
1443	  `.ddx/runs/<run-id>/` with `layer: 1` and the layer-1 extension
1444	  populated.
1445	- Given the run completes, when I run `ddx runs show <run-id>`, then I
1446	  see common fields, the layer-1 extension, requested `MinPower`/`MaxPower`,
1447	  actual model/power, requested passthrough constraints if supplied, and attachment
1448	  references.
1449	
1450	### US-091: Developer Inspects a Layer-2 Bead Attempt
1451	
1452	**As a** developer reviewing a bead attempt
1453	**I want** to see the attempt's worktree finalization and the child
1454	layer-1 invocations
1455	**So that** I can audit what the agent did inside the attempt
1456	
1457	**Acceptance Criteria:**
1458	- Given I invoke `ddx try <bead-id>`, then DDx writes one layer-2
1459	  record with `layer: 2` referencing one or more layer-1 run ids.
1460	- Given the attempt finalizes, when I run `ddx runs show <run-id>`,
1461	  then the record reports `merge` or `preserve` and surfaces the
1462	  evidence bundle.
1463	
1464	### US-092: Developer Drains the Queue and Audits the Drain
1465	
1466	**As a** developer running `ddx work`
1467	**I want** the loop to stop on a clearly named condition
1468	**So that** I can tell whether the queue was drained, blocked, deferred,
1469	spinning, or interrupted
1470	
1471	**Acceptance Criteria:**
1472	- Given I invoke `ddx work`, then DDx writes exactly one layer-3 record
1473	  per invocation referencing each child layer-2 attempt id.
1474	- Given I press Ctrl-C while `ddx work` or `ddx try` is running, then DDx
1475	  prints `Cancel received, shutting down gracefully` before cleanup begins.
1476	- Given the loop stops, then the layer-3 record's terminal disposition
1477	  is one of `drained`, `blocked`, `deferred`, `no_progress`, `signal`,
1478	  and the evaluation log names which condition fired and on which
1479	  iteration.
1480	
1481	### US-093: No-Progress Detection Stops a Spinning Loop
1482	
1483	**As a** developer running `ddx work`
1484	**I want** the loop to stop when consecutive attempts make no progress
1485	**So that** I do not burn budget on a pathological queue state
1486	
1487	**Acceptance Criteria:**
1488	- Given `N` consecutive valid implementation attempts produce no commit and no
1489	  merged side-effect (default `N = 3`, configurable), then the next
1490	  stop-condition evaluation triggers `no_progress` and the layer-3 record
1491	  terminates with that disposition.
1492	- Given a candidate is skipped by readiness, claim race, routing preflight,
1493	  quota/transport/auth/tool setup failure, review error, or operator-action
1494	  class, then that iteration does not increment the no-progress counter.
1495	- Given retry policy permits escalation before `no_progress` fires, when
1496	  a retry is scheduled, then the next layer-1 run raises `MinPower`
1497	  and records the actual model/power returned by the agent.
1498	- Given the original invocation supplied `--harness`, `--provider`, or
1499	  `--model`, then retries pass the same values through unchanged and DDx does
1500	  not inspect those values when choosing the next requested `MinPower`.
1501	- Given retry escalation would exceed the power available under hard
1502	  passthrough pins, then DDx stops with `blocked_by_passthrough_constraint` or
1503	  `agent_power_unsatisfied`, records the Fizeau-supplied evidence, and does not
1504	  mutate the pins or call any route-selection helper.
1505	
1506	### US-094: Cross-Layer Run History
1507	
1508	**As a** workflow tool
1509	**I want** to query run history across the task execution layers from one place
1510	**So that** I do not have to merge two on-disk shapes by hand
1511	
1512	**Acceptance Criteria:**
1513	- Given `run`, `try`, and `work` records exist, when a tool calls
1514	  `GET /api/runs?layer=...`, then it receives records of the requested
1515	  layer with consistent common fields.
1516	- Given a layer-3 record exists, then its child layer-2 attempt ids and
1517	  their child layer-1 run ids are reachable by following
1518	  `parent_run_id` pointers.
1519	
1520	### US-095: Artifact Regeneration Records Provenance
1521	
1522	**As a** consumer of the document graph
1523	**I want** generated artifacts to point back to the run that produced
1524	them
1525	**So that** staleness can be evaluated against generator inputs
1526	
1527	**Acceptance Criteria:**
1528	- Given I regenerate an artifact via `ddx artifact regenerate <id>`,
1529	  then the resulting run record has `produces_artifact: <id>`.
1530	- Given a generated artifact has been produced by a run, then
1531	  FEAT-007's `generated_by` edge resolves to that run id.
1532	
1533	### US-096: Operator Migrates From Legacy Run Trees
1534	
1535	**As a** repo operator adopting the unified substrate
1536	**I want** legacy `.ddx/exec-runs/` and `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/`
1537	records to remain inspectable during the migration window
1538	**So that** adopting `.ddx/runs/` does not strand prior evidence
1539	
1540	**Acceptance Criteria:**
1541	- Given a repository contains legacy run data, when DDx reads run
1542	  history, then legacy paths surface through the unified read API as
1543	  layer-1 / layer-2 records during the documented migration window.
1544	- Given the migration window closes, then writers target only
1545	  `.ddx/runs/` and a one-shot rewrite has normalized prior records into
1546	  the unified substrate.
1547	
1548	## CONTRACT-003 Amendment
1549	
1550	This feature requires two narrow read-only-amendment-adjacent changes
1551	to CONTRACT-003: write surfaces for `artifactRegenerate` (HTTP/MCP) and
1552	`runRequeue` (GraphQL). `runRequeue` does not invoke the execution service —
1553	it reopens the originating bead so the existing `ddx work` loop can claim it
1554	again — but it is still a write because it mutates bead state and appends an
1555	audit event (see "Re-queue audit event schema" above).
1556	No other write surfaces are added; in particular, layer-1, layer-2,
1557	and layer-3 invocation remain CLI-only.
1558	
1559	If FEAT-006 implementation reveals additional CONTRACT-003 gaps (e.g.,
1560	upstream Cobra root export details), those amendments are scoped to
1561	FEAT-006 and not to this feature.
1562	
1563	## Dependencies
1564	
1565	- FEAT-005 (Artifacts) — `produces_artifact` references artifact IDs
1566	- FEAT-006 (Fizeau consumer contract) — provides the layer-1 consumer-side wrapper
1567	  and CONTRACT-003 boundary
1568	- FEAT-007 (Doc Graph) — `generated_by` edges read `produces_artifact`
1569	  from run records
1570	- DDx CLI infrastructure (config loading, command factory)
1571	
1572	## Out of Scope
1573	
1574	- **Run kinds beyond `run`, `try`, and `work`** — comparison, replay, benchmark,
1575	  adversarial review, effort estimate, and similar workflows are skill
1576	  compositions, not new layers or new on-disk shapes.
1577	- **Content-aware supervisory loop decisions** — `ddx work` performs
1578	  mechanical queue drain only. Decisions like "comparison failed →
1579	  enqueue reconciliation beads" are skill / plugin / workflow-tool
1580	  territory.
1581	- **Server-side layer-1/2/3 invocation** — only `artifactRegenerate`
1582	  has a write endpoint in this feature.
1583	- **Workflow-specific action semantics** — phase routing, methodology
1584	  scoring, tool-specific issue closing — delegated to workflow tools.
1585	- **Workflow-specific autonomy semantics** — DDx owns the mechanical retry,
1586	  review, cooldown, and budget policy in this feature and ADR-024. Higher-level
1587	  methodology decisions such as "file reconciliation beads after a benchmark"
1588	  remain workflow-tool territory.
1589	- **Hosted run history storage** — records are repo-local and
1590	  file-backed.
1591	- **Separate domain-specific runtime stores** — there is one substrate.
1592

101:### Run-detail page

### Run-detail page

The shared run-detail page is the canonical deep surface for anything exposed
through the Runs tab.

- Trust boundary: anyone with project read access can inspect the raw prompt,
  response, stderr, and tool-call arguments attached to visible `try` and
  `run` records. The page does not redact those fields inside the project
  boundary.
- Evidence browser whitelist: the Evidence tab renders only `*.txt`, `*.md`,
  `manifest.json`, `prompt.md`, and `result.json` inline, and only when the
  file is 64 KB or smaller. Other files remain download-only. The evidence
  browser does not render Markdown as HTML.

- `layer=run` rows expand to AgentSession transcript / billing / cached
  tokens / stderr (the old Sessions detail).
- `layer=try` rows expand to the `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/` bundle
  metadata, check results, and verdict (the old Executions detail).
- `layer=work` rows expand to queue inputs and child run links.
1379:### Run resolver contract

### Run resolver contract

The canonical deep loader for a run detail surface is `run(id:)`. It returns
the run-layer fields used by FEAT-008:

- `prompt`
- `response`
- `stderr`
- `bundleFiles[]`

The tool-call resolver is the `toolCalls(first, after)` stream exposed via
`runToolCalls(id:, first:, after:)`. It returns the normalized tool-call
stream as a paginated `RunToolCallConnection`, where each node projects the
canonical drain-time `ToolCallEntry` shape:

- `tool`
- `input`
- `output`
- `duration_ms`
- `error`

`ToolCallEntry` is the normalized agent-side record of one tool execution. DDx
persists the stream at drain time so the run substrate can page the stored
sequence without rehydrating raw agent logs.

The `bundleFile(path)` lookup is exposed via `runBundleFile(id:, path:)`. It
is confined to the run's bundle root, rejects path traversal, absolute paths,
and symlink escapes, and inlines content only for the whitelist of small text
files documented by FEAT-008.

### Layer-to-substrate mapping for the Runs UI
